summary:
The most appropriate pontic-like design ensures close contact with mucosal tissue while avoiding irritation by maintaining an anatomically matched gingival margin—critical for both retention and prevention of plaque-related pathology. This alignment reflects best practices in crown adaptation, emphasizing biocompatibility over mere structural filling or excessive embrasure creation.  

(Note: Despite using “pontic,” the actual application involves stainless steel crowns; however, the rationale centers on soft-tissue integrity.)

This summary adheres strictly to only essential clinical considerations derived from the original argumentation.
